title: caltha palustris var. cornuta
intro: Caltha palustris var. cornuta, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Marsh Marigold can also help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Marsh Marigold can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
